about.hero1: EmpathAI is a safe space to process grief and emotional pain.
about.hero2: Built on responsible AI and backed by expert-led research.
about.aboutP1: EmpathAI is a research-driven wellbeing platform designed to support individuals through grief, emotional distress, and mental health challenges using responsible AI, peer support, and reflective tools.
about.aboutP2: Built under the guidance of Professor Elvira Pérez Vallejos, a global leader in digital mental health and AI ethics at the University of Nottingham, EmpathAI bridges cutting-edge technology with compassionate care. Professor Pérez Vallejos brings decades of interdisciplinary expertise in AI ethics, mental health, and co-produced research—ensuring that EmpathAI is built with users' dignity, privacy, and emotional wellbeing at its core.
about.feature1Desc: Our AI-powered chatbot provides gentle, grounded support during difficult times like bereavement, anxiety, or emotional overwhelm. It responds with empathy, never judgement.
about.feature2Desc: Write about your thoughts, memories, and emotions in a safe, private space. Journaling can be a powerful tool for grief processing and emotional clarity.
about.feature3Desc: Track habits and emotional patterns to better understand your wellbeing over time. Gentle prompts help you stay aware and in tune with your daily rhythm.
about.feature4Title: Writing Letters to your loved ones
about.feature4Desc: Express love, longing, or unresolved feelings through guided letter-writing rituals. A deeply healing way to reconnect and release.
about.feature5Desc: Ground yourself with short meditations designed for grief, calm, or emotional overwhelm. Learn to pause, breathe, and gently come back to the present.
about.feature6Desc: A gentle guide to planting a memorial tree or sapling in honor of a loved one. Create a living tribute and ritual that nurtures remembrance and growth.
about.feature7Desc: Small, science-backed suggestions to support mental and emotional health—whether you're just surviving the day or looking to rebuild slowly.
about.researchP1: EmpathAI is a project led by Professor Elvira Pérez Vallejos, Professor of Mental Health and Digital Technology — jointly appointed by the School of Computer Science and the School of Medicine at the University of Nottingham.
about.researchP2: Professor Pérez Vallejos is internationally recognised for her work on AI Ethics, and was recently named among the 100 Brilliant Women in AI Ethics (2025). Her work ensures EmpathAI reflects the highest standards in privacy, ethical AI, co-production, and digital mental health innovation.
about.privacyIntro: Your data is treated with the highest standards of care and security.
about.privacyItem1: All messages sent to the chatbot and between users are stored in encrypted form.
about.privacyItem2: Data is securely stored on servers within the University of Nottingham.
about.privacyItem3: We do not sell, share, or monetise your personal data. Ever.
about.whyP1: EmpathAI is more than just a tech tool—it's a compassionate companion during life's most vulnerable moments. Guided by leading experts in Responsible AI, our mission is to deliver emotionally intelligent support that is safe, inclusive, and grounded in real-world ethics.
about.whyP2: EmpathAI is part of a broader vision to ethically integrate AI into sensitive domains like grief support, end-of-life care, and mental health—ensuring that innovation never comes at the expense of humanity.
about.whyQuote: Empathy is not a feature. It's our foundation.

layout.taglineDefault: Healing begins with a single deep breath!
layout.taglineCommunities: We shall surf the grief waves together!
layout.taglineJournals: Your Inner Self, One Day at a Time
layout.taglineLetters: Your Words, Their Memory — A Personal Letter for Healing
layout.taglineHabits: And it all begins with small wins!
layout.taglineMindful: Find your peace, one breath at a time
layout.taglinePlant: A Seed of Hope, A Tree of Life
layout.taglineWellness: The best way out is always through!

faith.hero: Explore rituals and beliefs across religions to support you in grief.

christianity.c1Desc: Christians believe death is a passage to eternal life with God.
christianity.c1Details:
According to John 11:25-26:
"I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will live, even though he dies."
Reflect on this promise in prayer, trusting in the hope beyond sorrow.
christianity.c2Desc: Psalm 23 reassures God's guidance through life's darkest valleys.
christianity.c2Details:
"The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want..."
Recite each line slowly, visualizing green pastures and still waters to soothe your heart.
christianity.c3Desc: Light a candle to symbolize the light of Christ overcoming darkness.
christianity.c3Details:
Choose a quiet evening. Light a candle and whisper a prayer for your loved one.
Sit in silence, letting the flame remind you of eternal hope.
christianity.c4Desc: Gather with others to share prayers, scripture, and memories.
christianity.c4Details:
Attend or organize a small service: read favorite passages, sing hymns, and offer testimonies.
Community solidarity brings comfort and mutual support.

islam.c1Desc: A concise prayer seeking Allah's mercy on the departed.
islam.c1Details:
Recite:
Allahummaghfirli warhamhu wa'afihi wa'fu 'anhu.
Focus on sincerity, imagining the soul finding peace.
islam.c2Desc: The opening chapter offers comfort and guidance.
islam.c2Details:
Read slowly: "In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious..."
Ponder each attribute of mercy as a balm for grief.
islam.c3Desc: Pay respects and pray for the deceased's forgiveness.
islam.c3Details: Stand respectfully at the grave, raise hands in dua, and say prayers requesting pardon and peace.
islam.c4Desc: Give charity (sadaqah) for the benefit of the departed.
islam.c4Details:
Donate food, water, or support a cause.
Intend the reward be shared with the loved one's soul.

hinduism.c1Desc: Honoring ancestors through offerings and rituals.
hinduism.c1Details:
Offer rice, black sesame, and water while chanting your ancestor's name.
This connects you to the lineage and fosters healing.
hinduism.c2Desc: A powerful chant for healing and liberation.
hinduism.c2Details:
Chant: "Om Tryambakam Yajamahe..."
Repeat 108 times, focusing on each syllable to calm the mind.
hinduism.c3Desc: Lighting a lamp signifies removing darkness with light.
hinduism.c3Details:
Light an oil lamp in a safe place.
Reflect on inner light guiding you through grief.

buddhism.c1Desc: Cultivate loving-kindness for yourself and others.
buddhism.c1Details:
Silently repeat: "May I be safe. May I be peaceful..."
Extend these wishes to all beings, including your loved one.
buddhism.c2Desc: Perform good deeds to honor the departed's memory.
buddhism.c2Details:
Donate to temples or volunteer.
Dedicate your positive actions as merit for their peaceful rebirth.
buddhism.c3Desc: Use chanting and silent reflection together.
buddhism.c3Details:
Chant a short sutra, then sit in silence for several minutes.
Notice thoughts without attachment.

judaism.c1Desc: A prayer exalting the divine amidst loss.
judaism.c1Details: Recite in the congregation or privately: introduction, affirmation of holiness, and communal hope.
judaism.c2Desc: Seven days of mourning with community support.
judaism.c2Details:
Invite close family/friends to share stories.
Sit on low stools, cover mirrors, focus on remembrance.
judaism.c3Desc: Give to charity in memory of the departed.
judaism.c3Details:
Donate to a cause they valued.
This act of justice honors their legacy.

interfaith.c1Desc: A universal symbol of hope and remembrance.
interfaith.c1Details:
Light a candle in quiet reflection.
Speak your loved one's name and hold a moment of silence.
interfaith.c2Desc: Pause to be fully present with your grief.
interfaith.c2Details:
Set a timer for 5 minutes.
Sit comfortably, breathe deeply, and observe your emotions.
interfaith.c3Desc: Write memories on paper and place them in a jar.
interfaith.c3Details:
Encourage family/friends to add notes.
Read them later to celebrate their life.

wellness.hero: Simple, gentle practices to nourish your body and mind.
wellness.t1Desc: Try simple stretches or yoga poses that honor your body's pace. A 10-minute gentle flow can ease tension and boost mood.
wellness.t2Desc: Establish a wind-down ritual: dim lights, herbal tea, and a short journaling session. Even 20 minutes can improve sleep quality.
wellness.t3Desc: Blend a smoothie with bananas, spinach, and almonds. Prepare a warm broth or soup rich in vegetables to comfort body and mind.
wellness.t4Desc: Reach out to a friend or support group. Share a walk, a meal, or a phone call—small gestures build emotional support.
wellness.snack1: Oatmeal bowl with berries, honey, and flax seeds.
wellness.snack2: Avocado toast topped with cherry tomatoes and olive oil.
wellness.snack3: Warm turmeric milk with almond butter and cinnamon.
wellness.sleepDesc: Create a consistent bedtime: dim lights, avoid screens 30 minutes before sleep, and try 4-7-8 breathing to relax the nervous system.

meditation.hero: Cultivate calm and clarity during moments of loss with guided, restorative practices.
meditation.benefit1: Reduces anxiety by engaging the parasympathetic system.
meditation.benefit2: Helps regulate intense emotions and cravings.
meditation.benefit3: Enhances focus, making daily tasks feel more manageable.
meditation.benefit4: Creates a gentle anchor in the present moment.
meditation.benefitsDesc: Each breath is an opportunity to ground yourself, to notice and release what no longer serves you. Over time, this practice builds resilience and compassionate awareness you can carry into everyday life.
meditation.p1Step1: Sit comfortably with spine straight, feet flat on the floor.
meditation.p1Step2: Place one hand on your belly, the other on your chest.
meditation.p1Step3: Inhale through your nose for 4 counts, feeling the diaphragm expand.
meditation.p1Step4: Exhale through pursed lips for 6 counts, releasing tension.
meditation.p2Step1: Lie down or sit with eyes closed in a quiet space.
meditation.p2Step2: Bring attention to your toes, notice sensations or tension.
meditation.p2Step3: Gradually move awareness upward: feet to legs to hips to back to shoulders to arms to neck to head.
meditation.p2Step4: Breathe into areas of tension and release with each exhale.
meditation.p3Step1: Find a flat, safe path where you can walk uninterrupted.
meditation.p3Step2: Begin walking slowly, feeling the lift, movement, and placement of each foot.
meditation.p3Step3: Coordinate your natural breath with your steps (e.g., 3 steps inhale, 3 steps exhale).
meditation.p3Step4: Maintain soft gaze and relaxed posture.
meditation.scriptText: "Close your eyes and take three deep, intentional breaths. As you inhale, envision drawing in peace; as you exhale, release tension and stress. Gently return your focus to the rise and fall of your breath whenever your mind wanders."
meditation.journal1: What sensations did you notice during practice?
meditation.journal2: How did your emotions shift from start to finish?
meditation.journal3: What thoughts arose, and what did they teach you?

sapling.hero1: Create a living tribute to a loved one.
sapling.step1: Choose a native or symbolic tree species suited to your climate and space.
sapling.step2: Prepare the planting hole: twice as wide as the root ball, same depth.
sapling.step3: Gently loosen roots and position sapling upright in the center.
sapling.step4: Backfill soil, firm gently, and water deeply to settle the roots.
sapling.step5: Stake if needed, and mulch around the base to retain moisture.
sapling.ritualDesc: Honor your loved one by writing a dedication on a small plaque, tying a ribbon, or speaking a few words before planting. This mindful ritual deepens the emotional connection and seals your intention.
sapling.aftercareDesc: Water your sapling deeply twice a week for the first six months, more during hot, dry spells. Apply mulch yearly, prune dead branches in winter, and record height and leaf development in a journal or photo log.
